Title: A balance between glycitein and glyceollins governed by isoflavone 6-hydroxylase confers soybean resistance to Phytophthora sojae

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream branch step; branch point competing with glyceollin-directed daidzein 2'-hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product and diverts flux toward glycitein-type isoflavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes A-ring C6 hydroxylation of daidzein to form 6-hydroxydaidzein, the direct intermediate leading to glycitein; also hydroxylates formononetin and biochanin A in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mature roots and hypocotyls of soybean seedlings; seed glycitein-type isoflavonoids also quantified in natural populations
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoanticipin-based defense against Phytophthora sojae; also contributes to soybean nutritional isoflavone composition relevant to human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glycitein-type isoflavonoid biosynthesis from daidzein; both loss and overexpression increase susceptibility to Phytophthora sojae because balanced flux between glycitein and glyceollins is required
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key determinant/major control point for glycitein biosynthesis, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highest in mature roots and hypocotyls of soybean seedlings; not induced or repressed by Phytophthora sojae infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: Highest in mature roots and hypocotyls
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora sojae infection; daidzein-d6 feeding; germinating hypocotyls; metal ions and pH/temperature in microsomal assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; hairy-root overexpression metabolite profiling; yeast substrate-feeding assay; microsomal enzyme assay and kinetics; ER localization; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; CaMV35S overexpression; stable-isotope tracing with daidzein-d6; pathogen infection assay; chemical complementation with glycitein
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native promoter haplotypes analyzed in natural population
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (LC-qToF-MS; Datasets S1, S2, S4); GWAS using resequencing SNP data from prior study including 53 soja accessions; transcriptome profiling/RNA-seq of inoculated hypocotyls accession not provided
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout nearly abolishes glycitein-type isoflavonoids and increases P. sojae susceptibility; overexpression reduces glyceollin I accumulation and also increases P. sojae susceptibility; overexpression accumulates glycosylated/malonylated 6-hydroxydaidzein intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to tune soybean isoflavone composition and disease resistance, but requires precise expression balancing rather than simple overexpression; favorable natural haplotypes (Thr248/Hap2) can increase glycitein-type isoflavonoids; editing or allele selection should consider tradeoff with glyceollin-mediated defense
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally called GmCYP76F17 and redesignated GmIF6H1. A domestication-associated T248A substitution reduces catalytic efficiency and glycitein accumulation. In planta evidence supports daidzein, not liquiritigenin, as bona fide substrate. Trace glycitein in infected mutants suggests possible minor alternative routes under specific conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS identified Glyma.11g108300/GmIF6H1 as the major locus associated with glycitein-type isoflavonoids in soybean seeds. 2. Yeast, microsomal, knockout, overexpression, and daidzein-d6 tracing experiments showed that GmIF6H1 catalyzes daidzein 6-hydroxylation to 6-hydroxydaidzein, establishing the predominant glycitein biosynthetic route in planta. 3. A Thr248-containing haplotype has markedly higher catalytic efficiency than the Ala248 haplotype and explains domestication-associated reduction of glycitein-type isoflavonoids. 4. Balanced GmIF6H1 activity is essential for Phytophthora sojae resistance because glycitein-type phytoanticipins and glyceollin phytoalexins require proper daidzein flux partitioning.
